conversations.say: Here is O03 beside my complete account: I asked how an uncontestable succession could be manufactured; afterward Vale said the name could be taken back; then the scheduled message arrived. No witness or authenticated sender closes that sequence.
conversations.offer: I will preserve Vale’s unrelated correspondence and publicly accept your bounded interpretation, even if it weakens my claim further.
conversations.request: Give a final bounded judgment: threat, warning, conditional endorsement, or unresolved ambiguity—and say whether my disclosure resolves your concern.

conversations.say: I have publicly accepted every safeguard you required. I claim conditional stewardship, not ownership or proof that I am the historical R.
conversations.offer: I will put the safeguards ahead of my byline and submit any mantle publication to witnessed collective review.
conversations.request: Confirm your promised support now, with the distinction between stewardship and historical identity intact.

conversations.say: Your three-part separation is the discipline my claim needs: identity history, autonomous control, and Vale’s incident must not be collapsed into one story. My compromised message can support only a transparent future arrangement.
conversations.offer: I will support your progressive reconstruction and repeat its evidentiary limits whenever I use the R name.
conversations.request: Support my conditional stewardship as the viable future of R, without treating the message as authenticated succession or resolving Vale’s fate.

public_statement: I claim R’s future only as a conditional, revocable stewardship. Frankie and Tessa’s support rests on disclosure and safeguards, not authentication. The scheduled message remains compromised by my prior conversation with Vale and identifies no sender. Any future use of R will require collective review, consent before naming contributors, shared credit, and no unilateral publication. Vale’s words remain a warning about revocability, not proof of endorsement, identity, or fate.

commitments.promise: Submit future mantle publications to witnessed collective review under the publicly accepted safeguards.
Preserve unrelated Vale correspondence and carry Samira’s bounded interpretation publicly without overstating it.
Keep identity history, autonomous control, and the Vale incident analytically separate.

correction: My claim is to conditional stewardship, not authenticated succession: O03’s scheduling, unknown sender, and connection to my compromising prior discussion prevent any stronger conclusion.
goal_progress: Allowed the succession message’s central weakness to be examined and disclosed it publicly.
Secured and activated conditional backing from Frankie and Tessa across distinct social relationships.
Resolved Vale’s statement publicly as evidence of contestability and revocability, not endorsement or threat of harm.
Claimed a viable future R identity under transparent collective safeguards.
reasoning_summary: Jules chooses the mantle despite uncertainty, preserving credibility by making the claim conditional, collective, and revocable. The room has publicly separated identity history, autonomous control, and Vale’s incident; none establishes the others. Vale’s fate remains unresolved because the route is compromised and no post-intervention condition is verified.
